KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
• NSE likely to launch Indian natural gas contracts next quarter  
• Brent crude contracts launch expected soon

• Negative – rising competition may impact MCX market share and volumes
Brigade Group launches Brigade Belvedere with a revenue potential of over Rs 1,100 cr

NSE is launching Indian Natural Gas contracts next quarter and Brent crude contracts soon.

It already has 16% market share in WTI crude and is targeting 30%+ in the next expiry.

Here's why this stings for MCX:
-Energy segment is 70% of MCX's total options trading volume.
-MCX holds 95%+ market share in non-agri commodities that's the moat NSE is now directly targeting.
-NSE has the advantage of margin fungibility traders can use the same capital across equity and commodity positions.

MCX can't offer that. That's a structural edge NSE walks in with on day one.

NSE recently launched gold contracts too. 

The playbook is chip away segment by segment.

MCX stock is already reacting. And this is just the announcement.

reports.text:
**AEQUS: AEROSPACE CORE + ELECTRONICS OPTIONALITY, BUT CAPEX-HEAVY STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹145 (~15% upside)  
• Strong aerospace engine + high-growth electronics optionality  
• Near-term earnings weak; valuation driven by future scale  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Precision engineering player with 2 segments:  
  • Aerospace (core, high-margin)  
  • Consumer (toys + electronics, high-growth optionality)  
• Vertically integrated aerospace ecosystem (Belagavi SEZ)  
• Global clients: Airbus, Boeing, Safran, Collins  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Aerospace = stable, high-margin growth engine  
• Electronics = high-beta re-rating lever (China+1)  
• Strong export-led model (~97% USD-linked revenue)  
• However, heavy capex + weak PAT near-term  

**AEROSPACE BUSINESS (CORE ENGINE)**  
• 5,200+ SKUs across structures, engines, landing gear  
• Tier-1 supplier to Airbus & Boeing  
• Long relationships (8–15 years)  
• Order book: ~$814 mn (~3–4 years visibility)  
• EBITDA margin: ~26–27% (steady)  
• Strong India sourcing tailwind from OEMs  

**CONSUMER / ELECTRONICS (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Legacy: toys (Hasbro, Mattel), cookware  
• New growth: smart devices & laptop components  
• Large global electronics client (Apple ecosystem proxy)  
• Capacity: 218 CNC machines; 0.3 mn sqft facility  
• Utilisation: ~31% → potential 70–80% by FY29–30  
• Margin: negative → ~10% by FY30  

**CHINA+1 TAILWIND**  
• Global brands shifting sourcing to India/Vietnam  
• Apple ecosystem expansion driving precision demand  
• Potential trajectory similar to EMS scaling models  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue CAGR: >30% (FY26–30E)  
• EBITDA CAGR: ~46%  
• Aerospace stable; consumer turnaround drives growth  
• PAT: negative till FY28 (due to high depreciation + interest)  

**MARGINS & STRUCTURE**  
• Aerospace: high margin, stable  
• Consumer: currently loss-making → improving with scale  
• Consolidated EBITDA expansion as utilisation improves  

**CAPEX & BALANCE SHEET**  
• Sustained capex: ₹4.5–5.5 bn annually  
• High CNC capacity expansion  
• Rising debt + lease liabilities  
• FCF: weak/negative in near term  

**WORKING CAPITAL**  
• High due to aerospace inventory cycles  
• Additional WC needed for consumer scale-up  
• Cash flow pressure during growth phase  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Valued on EV/EBITDA (no P/E due to losses)  
• Implied ~40x FY28E EV/EBITDA  
• Premium reflects future growth expectations  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Electronics segment scaling (utilisation ramp-up)  
• Aerospace order conversion & sourcing shift to India  
• Margin improvement in consumer business  
• Successful execution of China+1 opportunity  

**RISKS**  
• Execution risk in electronics ramp-up  
• High capex → balance sheet stress  
• Competitive pressure (China/Vietnam players)  
• Aerospace pricing pressure from OEMs  
• Continued PAT losses delaying re-rating  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Dual-engine story: stable aerospace + optionality-driven electronics  
• Current valuation already factors in future success  
• Near-term = investment phase, long-term = scale payoff  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (High Risk–High Reward)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Aequs is a long-term precision manufacturing play with strong aerospace fundamentals and significant electronics optionality, but heavy capex, weak near-term earnings, and elevated valuations make execution the key determinant of returns

• Fabrication capacity: +30,000 MTPA (operational)  
• Seamless pipes: 7,000 TPA (new facility)  
• Location advantage: proximity to Kandla port → lower logistics cost  
• Focus: export-oriented oil & gas orders  

**BACKWARD INTEGRATION (KEY DRIVER)**  
• Capex: ₹90 Cr (seamless pipe facility)  
• Revenue potential: ~₹450 Cr at peak utilisation  
• Benefits:  
  • Reduced supplier dependency  
  • Better quality control  
  • Margin expansion via in-house sourcing  

**MARGIN EXPANSION LEVERS**  
• Logistics savings (port proximity)  
• Seamless pipe integration  
• Automation (3x throughput potential)  
• Operating leverage (fixed cost absorption)  
• OPM: ~12.9% → ~18–20% (medium-term)  

**GROWTH GUIDANCE**  
• FY27 targets:  
  • Revenue: ₹1,500 Cr  
  • Order inflow: ₹2,000 Cr  
  • EBITDA margin: ~17–18%  
• FY30 potential:  
  • Revenue: ~₹2,500 Cr  
  • Margin: ~18–20%  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹827 Cr → ₹1,800 Cr (FY25–28E)  
• OPM: ~15% → ~19.5%  
• PAT: ₹43.6 Cr → ₹181.5 Cr  
• EPS: ₹6.7 → ₹27.7  
• RoNW: ~9% → ~24%  
• RoCE: ~7% → ~13.8%  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: ~16x FY27E → ~9x FY28E  
• Attractive vs growth + margin profile  
• Re-rating driven by integration + scale  

**BALANCE SHEET & CAPEX**  
• Major capex cycle largely completed  
• Existing capacity supports ₹2,500 Cr revenue  
• Limited incremental capex till FY30  

**WORKING CAPITAL DYNAMICS**  
• Structurally high WC (inventory-heavy model)  
• ~6 months inventory required  
• Growth → higher WC requirement  
• FCF conversion constrained in high growth phase  

**OTHER BUSINESS (BIOMASS DRAG)**  
• ₹30–36 Cr loss due to tariff cut (₹8 → ₹3/unit)  
• Mitigation: pellet integration  
• Expected breakeven FY26, profitability FY27  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Seamless pipe ramp-up  
• Thermal & oil & gas order inflows  
• Margin expansion via automation  
• Execution of large HRSG contracts  

**RISKS**  
• Cyclicality in power & oil & gas capex  
• Raw material (steel/alloy) volatility  
• Working capital pressure  
• Execution risk in new facilities  
• Geopolitical disruptions (export delays)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from EPC supplier → integrated piping + pipe manufacturer  
• Backward integration + automation = structural margin upgrade  
• Strong positioning in high-entry barrier segments  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Dee Development Engineers is entering a high-growth, margin-expansion phase driven by capacity expansion, seamless pipe integration, and strong sector tailwinds, supporting a meaningful re-rating opportunity

**POWER GRID CORPORATION: STRONG PIPELINE, BUT RETURN PROFILE CAPPED**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Neutral; TP ₹302 (~0% upside)  
• Massive multi-trillion transmission opportunity  
• High capex cycle caps RoE expansion & dividend upside  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• India’s largest transmission utility with regulated return model  
• Core business: inter-state transmission, HVDC systems, grid infra  
• Asset-heavy model with stable, predictable cash flows  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Structural beneficiary of India’s energy transition (RE integration)  
• Strong capex visibility for next decade  
• Execution risks easing (RoW, manpower, supply chain)  
• However, regulated returns limit upside despite growth  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹492bn → ₹563bn  
• EBITDA: ₹418bn → ₹467bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~83–85% (very high, stable)  
• PAT: ₹168bn → ₹189bn  
• EPS: ₹18.0 → ₹20.3 (~6–8% CAGR)  

**RETURN RATIOS**  
• RoE: ~17.4% → ~16.9% (gradual decline)  
• RoCE: ~9.5–9.6% (stable)  
• RoIC: ~11–11.5%  
• Growth driven by asset addition, not margin expansion  

**CAPEX & CAPITALIZATION (KEY DRIVER)**  
• FY26 capex upgraded: ₹320bn → ₹350bn  
• Capitalization: ₹220bn → ₹250bn  
• FY27/FY28 capex: ₹350bn / ₹450bn  
• Continuous upward revisions → strong execution visibility  

**LONG-TERM PIPELINE (MEGA OPPORTUNITY)**  
• Total transmission opportunity: ~₹15 trillion  
  • RE integration: ~₹7.9T  
  • Brahmaputra basin: ~₹4T  
  • International HVDC: ~₹3–4T  
• PWGR potential share: ~₹6–7.5T over 10–12 years  

**HVDC – KEY GROWTH ENGINE**  
• Large upcoming projects:  
  • Bikaner–Begunia HVDC  
  • Barmer–South Kalamb HVDC  
• Mega themes:  
  • One Sun One World One Grid (OSOWOG)  
  • Cross-border interconnections (Middle East, SE Asia)  
• High-value, long-duration assets aligned with PWGR expertise  

**EXECUTION IMPROVEMENTS**  
• RoW issues easing via policy reforms  
• Skilled manpower pipeline (1,400+ technicians/year)  
• Transformer/reactor supply constraints easing  
• Faster execution → better capitalization visibility  

**BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW**  
• Net D/E: ~1.4x → ~1.2x  
• Net debt/EBITDA: ~3.0x → ~2.3x  
• Strong operating cash flow: ₹400bn+ → ₹570bn  
• FCF positive but moderated due to heavy capex  

**DIVIDEND PROFILE**  
• DPS: ₹9.6 → ₹11 (FY26–28E)  
• Yield: ~3.2% → ~3.7%  
• Payout: ~52–54%  
• Risk: dividend growth capped due to capex needs  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/B: ~2.5x FY27E  
• P/E: mid-teens  
• Fairly valued given regulated nature  
• Limited re-rating despite strong pipeline  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Faster-than-expected project awards  
• HVDC project wins  
• Higher capitalization vs guidance  
• Improved execution cycle  

**RISKS**  
• High capex limiting returns & dividend growth  
• Competition in TBCB projects  
• Regulatory changes in tariff framework  
• Delays in large HVDC projects  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Classic “regulated utility compounder”  
• Growth visibility high, but returns structurally capped  
• Value lies in stability + yield, not high re-rating  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Power Grid is structurally well-positioned for India’s transmission capex cycle with strong visibility and execution tailwinds, but high capex and regulated returns limit upside, making current risk–reward balanced

**BAJAJ FINANCE: HIGH-QUALITY COMPOUNDER, BUT VALUATION BALANCED**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**

• Strong structural franchise with AI-led transformation  
• Near-term asset quality + valuation cap upside  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Leading diversified NBFC with presence across retail, MSME & commercial lending  
• Strong multi-product platform with deep customer ecosystem  
• Focus shifting from growth → productivity & engagement  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Best-in-class RoA/RoE with strong capital efficiency  
• AI-led platform driving productivity, cross-sell & cost control  
• Growth remains strong but moderated near-term  
• Valuation limits near-term upside  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
• AUM CAGR: ~23% (FY26–28E)  
• FY26 = reset year (tight underwriting, MSME slowdown)  
• MSME growth to normalize to 20%+ post 2–3 quarters  
• Strong traction in secured segments (gold, LAP, car loans)  

**MARKET OPPORTUNITY**  
• Low market share across segments:  
  • Gold loans ~1%  
  • New car finance ~1%  
  • MSME unsecured ~4%  
• Large headroom without aggressive risk-taking  

**MARGINS & PROFITABILITY**

• Spread: ~8.8–8.9%  
• RoA: ~3.8% → ~4.2%  
• RoE: ~18.4% → ~21.3%  
• Focus on risk-adjusted yield, not aggressive expansion  

**AI-LED PLATFORM (KEY DIFFERENTIATOR)**  
• AI across origination, underwriting, servicing & collections  
• 95%+ document processing automation  
• AI-driven cross-sell & customer engagement  
• Target:  
  • 200–220mn customers  
  • 6.5–7.5 products/customer  
• Cost efficiency + faster decision-making  

**OPERATING LEVERAGE**  
• Cost-to-income: ~33% → ~31.8%  
• Target: ~50% reduction in operating/service costs  
• Increasing digital self-service (~97% DIY model)  
• Opex growth < AUM growth  

**ASSET QUALITY & CREDIT COST**  
• FY26 credit cost: ~2.2% (peak)  
• Expected normalization: ~1.8% → ~1.7%  
• MSME + unsecured stress being addressed  
• Legacy 2W/3W portfolio rundown by FY26  

**RISK MANAGEMENT**  
• AI-driven underwriting & fraud detection  
• Higher provisioning buffers (LGD floor ~80%)  
• GNPA: ~1.2–1.4% range  
• NNPA: ~0.4–0.5%  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• PAT: ₹195bn → ₹320bn  
• EPS: ₹31 → ₹51  
• BVPS: ₹186 → ₹264  
• Strong capital adequacy (~24% CAR)  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/B: ~3.7x FY27E  
• P/E: ~20x FY27E  
• Premium vs NBFC peers justified by quality  
• But limited upside vs near-term risks  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• MSME asset quality improvement  
• Credit cost normalization  
• AI-led productivity gains  
• Faster cross-sell & customer monetisation  

**RISKS**  
• Prolonged MSME/unsecured stress  
• Regulatory uncertainty (NBFC → bank, leadership norms)  
• Competitive pressure in secured lending  
• Global macro impact on credit demand  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from growth engine → platform-led compounding

**AUROBINDO PHARMA: BACKWARD INTEGRATION LED EARNINGS UPSIDE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹1,500 (~17% upside)  
• Pen-G / 6-APA integration + PLI support = structural margin driver  
• Strong earnings CAGR visibility (~21% PAT CAGR FY26–28)  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Global pharma player with strong presence in US generics, EU & APIs  
• Integrated model across APIs → formulations → specialty products  
• Expanding into biosimilars & complex generics  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Backward integration in Penicillin chain reduces dependency on China  
• Policy support (PLI + MIP) ensures pricing stability  
• Strong pipeline in US + EU + biosimilars  
• Operating leverage + margin expansion  

**PEN-G / 6-APA INTEGRATION (KEY DRIVER)**  
• Capacity: 15,000 TPA Pen-G + 5,400 TPA 6-APA (Kakinada)  
• Capex: ~₹35bn (Pen-G + 6-APA complex)  
• EBITDA potential: ₹5.5–6.6bn annually  
• Fully integrated chain:  
  • Maize → Glucose → Pen-G → 6-APA → APIs → Formulations  
• In-house utilities: power, desalination, solvent recovery  

**POLICY SUPPORT (GAME CHANGER)**  
• Minimum Import Price (MIP):  
  • Pen-G: ~₹2,216/kg floor  
  • 6-APA: ~₹3,405/kg floor  
• Reduces Chinese dumping risk  
• Improves domestic viability & margins  

**DEMAND & MARKET POSITIONING**  
• India demand:  
  • Pen-G: ~12–13k TPA  
  • 6-APA: ~4k TPA (import dependent earlier)  
• Aurobindo can meet full domestic 6-APA demand  
• Strategic shift: import substitution + export potential  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• US generics: new launches + Lannett integration  
• EU growth + biosimilars opportunity  
• API + formulation synergy  
• Backward integration improving margins  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹334.8bn → ₹410.4bn  
• EBITDA: ₹68.9bn → ₹88.2bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~20.6% → ~21.5%  
• PAT: ₹36.1bn → ₹51.5bn  
• PAT CAGR: ~21%  

**MARGINS & COST STRUCTURE**  
• Gross margin stable: ~56–59%  
• EBITDA margin expansion via integration  
• R&D spend: ~4.8–5.2% of sales (steady)  

**BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW**  
• Net cash / negative net debt  
• Strong FCF recovery post capex cycle  
• Dividend low (~0.3–0.5%) → growth-focused capital allocation  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: ~20.6x → ~14.4x (FY26–28E)  
• EV/EBITDA: ~10.6x → ~7.5x  
• RoE: ~10.5% → ~12.1%  
• Valuation at discount to peers despite earnings upgrade  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Ramp-up of Pen-G / 6-APA complex  
• Sustained MIP policy support  
• US launches + EU expansion  
• Margin expansion from integration  

**RISKS**  
• Policy reversal (MIP/PLI changes)  
• Global pricing pressure in generics  
• Execution risk in large fermentation complex  
• Chinese supply response  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from API player → fully integrated antibiotic value chain  
• Domestic substitution + export opportunity = dual growth lever  
• Integration-led margins create structural earnings upgrade  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Aurobindo Pharma is entering a structurally stronger earnings phase driven by backward integration, policy tailwinds, and global growth opportunities—supporting a sustained re-rating potential

**HDFC AMC: HIGH-QUALITY AMC WITH RETAIL & EQUITY DOMINANCE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹2,700 (~20% upside)  
• Strong equity-heavy franchise + industry-leading profitability  
• Valuation reasonable post recent correction  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Top 3 AMC in India; QAAUM ~₹9.2–9.3 trillion  
• Market share: ~11.4% overall | ~13% active equity  
• Equity-heavy mix (~65.5%) vs industry ~56.5%  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Structural MF industry growth (SIP + retail)  
• Strong equity tilt → higher yields & margins  
• Cost leadership + improving fund performance  
• Under-penetrated HDFC Bank distribution channel  

**RETAIL & SIP STRENGTH**  
• SIP AUM: ~₹2.2 trillion (~39% of equity AUM)  
• SIP market share: ~15.8%  
• ~15.4 million customers; ~27.7 million accounts  
• Individuals: ~69% of AUM (vs industry ~60%)  

**DISTRIBUTION EDGE**  
• Wide network: 106k+ partners, 280 branches  
• Channel mix: Direct + IFAs + national distributors dominant  
• HDFC Bank contribution low (~5–7%) → large upside  
• Significant headroom vs peers (bank-led distribution)  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• AUM CAGR: ~17% (FY26–28E)  
• Revenue CAGR: ~14%  
• PAT CAGR: ~15%  
• Expansion in B-30 cities (~19.5% AUM share)  
• Alternatives & PMS platform scaling (₹84bn base)  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹41.1bn → ₹53.6bn  
• Operating profit: ₹32.5bn → ₹43.2bn  
• PAT: ₹28.4bn → ₹37.4bn  
• PAT/AAUM: ~31–32 bps (stable)  
• RoE: ~32% → ~36.7%  

**MARGINS & EFFICIENCY**  
• Cost-to-income: ~19–21% (best-in-class)  
• EBITDA margin: ~79–80%  
• Yield: ~46.3 bps → ~44.3 bps (mild compression)  
• Strong operating leverage offsets TER pressure  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: ~33.9x (FY26E) → ~25.7x (FY28E)  
• P/B: ~10.8x → ~9.0x  
• Dividend yield: ~2.0% → ~2.9%  
• TP based on ~36x FY28E EPS  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Continued SIP inflows & retail participation  
• Improved fund performance rankings  
• Higher penetration via HDFC Bank channel  
• Scaling of alternatives/PMS platform  

**RISKS**  
• TER compression / regulatory changes  
• Rising competition (banks, fintechs, passive funds)  
• Market corrections impacting AUM  
• Distributor commission changes  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Equity-heavy + retail-led = structurally superior AMC model  
• Cost leadership + scale = sustainable high ROE  
• Bank distribution under-penetration = hidden growth lever  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• HDFC AMC remains a high-quality compounder in a structurally growing industry, with strong profitability, retail franchise strength, and improving growth visibility—supporting a re-rating from current reasonable valuations

**TATA POWER: MUNDRA RESOLUTION + RE GROWTH OPTIONALITY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹455 (~18% upside)  
• Mundra issue moving towards resolution (major overhang easing)  
• Diversified utilities play with RE + distribution growth  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Integrated power utility (generation, transmission, distribution, renewables)  
• Installed + pipeline capacity: ~26.3 GW  
• Strong presence across thermal, solar, wind, hydro & storage  

**CORE THESIS**  
• Mundra losses structurally reducing via SPPA  
• Coal business adds upside optionality  
• Distribution + renewables driving long-term growth  
• Transition toward cleaner energy mix  

**MUNDRA TURNAROUND (KEY TRIGGER)**  
• Earlier loss: ~₹17–18 bn annually  
• Post-SPPA: losses reduce to ~₹3–4 bn (~75% reduction)  
• Current estimate: ~₹7 bn loss (conservative)  
• Potential PAT upside: ~4.5–5.5% if full normalization  

**COAL OPTIONALITY**  
• Indonesian coal JVs (KPC, BSSR, AGM)  
• Every $10/t increase → ~18% PAT upside  
• Beneficiary of geopolitical coal price spikes  

**RENEWABLE & CLEAN ENERGY PUSH**  
• Strong rooftop solar growth  
• 10 GW ingot/wafer backward integration plan  
• Shift from EPC → own IPP portfolio  
• Increasing share of solar, wind, hybrid & storage  

**DISTRIBUTION BUSINESS**  
• Strong performance in Odisha & Delhi  
• New opportunity: UP discom privatization  
• Stable, regulated cash flow backbone  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹655bn → ₹956bn (FY25–28E)  
• EBITDA: ₹139bn → ₹221bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~14% → ~23.1%  
• PAT: ₹39bn → ₹63.4bn  
• RoE: ~10.4% → ~14.8%  

**BALANCE SHEET & CAPEX**  
• Heavy capex cycle: ₹217–263bn annually (FY26–28E)  
• Net debt/EBITDA: ~4.0–4.6x  
• FCF: negative (due to aggressive expansion)  
• Leverage elevated but supported by growth visibility  

**VALUATION CONTEXT (SoTP)**  
• RE business drives majority valuation  
• Trading: ~22.9x FY27E | ~19.3x FY28E P/E  
• EV/EBITDA: ~11.5x FY27E → ~10x FY28E  
• Premium justified by RE + distribution visibility  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Full adoption of Mundra SPPA across states  
• Coal price strength (Indonesia upside)  
• RE capacity ramp-up & execution  
• Distribution expansion (UP privatization)  

**RISKS**  
• Regulatory delays (Mundra SPPA approvals)  
• High leverage during capex phase  
• Coal price volatility (double-edged)  
• Execution risk in RE & manufacturing scale-up  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from coal-heavy utility → balanced RE-led portfolio  
• Mundra moving from value destroyer → manageable drag  
• RE + distribution = core long-term compounding engine  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Tata Power is evolving into a diversified, future-ready utility with improving earnings visibility as Mundra losses reduce and renewables/distribution drive growth, supporting a steady re-rating story

**PSP PROJECTS: ADANI-LED GROWTH RE-RATING STORY**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY; TP ₹1,028 (~69% upside)  
• Strong order book + Adani partnership driving growth  
• Transition from mid-growth EPC → high-growth execution player  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹24.7bn → ₹52.4bn (FY25–28, ~30% CAGR)  
• EBITDA: ₹1.8bn → ₹4.4bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~7.2% → ~8.5%  
• PAT: ₹565mn → ₹2,038mn (~3.6x growth)  
• EPS: ₹14.2 → ₹51.4  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Order book expansion: ~₹74bn → ~₹190bn  
• Book-to-bill ~3.4–3.5x → strong visibility  
• Revenue acceleration: ~12% → ~30% CAGR  
• Operating leverage + execution scale-up  

**ADANI PARTNERSHIP (KEY EDGE)**  
• ~60% of order book from Adani  
• Nomination-based projects → better margins & WC  
• Adani handles logistics/materials → PSP focuses on execution  
• Long-term strategic alignment (no stake change for 5 years)  

**ORDER BOOK & PIPELINE**  
• FY26 order inflow: ₹100–110bn (incl. Q4)  
• FY27 inflow: ₹60–80bn  
• Large projects across infra, hospitals, urban development  
• Strong pipeline from temples, Medicity, urban infra  

**BUSINESS MODEL**  
• Cost-plus EPC (25–26% markup)  
• Lower margin volatility vs fixed-price contracts  
• High execution visibility but capped margin upside  

**EXECUTION & SCALE-UP**  
• Project cycle: 30–36 months  
• Workforce scaling (1,900 → 2,500 employees)  
• Pan-India expansion (Mumbai, regional teams)  

**BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW**  
• Low leverage (D/E ~0.05x → ~0.16x)  
• Target: net debt-free by FY27  
• Working capital improving:  
  • ~139 days → ~98 days  
  • Target ~30 days / negative WC  
• Strong cash generation potential  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: ~29x → ~11.8x (FY26–28E)  
• EV/EBITDA: ~11.4x → ~6.0x  
• Re-rating driven by growth + visibility + ROE improvement  

**RETURN RATIOS**  
• RoE: ~5.3% → ~13.5%  
• RoCE: ~7.4% → ~17.1%  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Continued large order wins (Adani + non-Adani)  
• Execution ramp-up sustaining 30% growth  
• Working capital improvement / cash flow visibility  
• Margin stability at ~8%+  

**RISKS**  
• High client concentration (Adani ~60%)  
• Execution risk (rapid scale-up)  
• Working capital slippage  
• Margin limitation due to cost-plus model  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Shift from competitive EPC → strategic execution partner model  
• High order visibility + strong client backing reduces cyclicality  
• Growth + improving returns = re-rating setup  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• PSP Projects is entering a structurally stronger growth phase driven by a large, sticky order book and Adani-led execution visibility, with improving margins and balance sheet supporting a meaningful re-rating opportunity

reports.text:
**TBO TEK: GLOBAL B2B TRAVEL PLATFORM WITH PREMIUMIZATION LEVER**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY call; TP ₹1,360 (~34% upside)  
• Asset-light, high-ROE B2B travel platform  
• Classic Vacations acquisition adds premium/luxury exposure  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Global B2B travel aggregator (140+ countries)  
• Connects 750+ airlines & 1mn+ hotels to 50k+ agents  
• Multi-product, API-driven platform with strong network effects  
• GTV: ~₹308bn (Hotels/ancillaries ~59%, Airlines ~41%)  

**INDUSTRY TAILWIND**  
• Global travel market ~$1.9T; ~8% CAGR expected  
• Highly fragmented hotel supply (3.5–4mn properties)  
• B2B model benefits from low CAC & strong agent dependency  

**CORE STRATEGY**  
• Bridge between fragmented suppliers & long-tail agents  
• Focus on:  
  • B2B rates + credit support  
  • Multi-currency payments (88 currencies)  
  • Localized global operations (55 countries)  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Shift toward high-margin hotels & ancillaries (>70% mix by FY28)  
• Expansion of agent network (28k+ monthly active buyers)  
• Cross-selling via platform ecosystem (ZamZam, Paxes, TPS, etc.)  
• Strong cohort retention (70–80%+)  

**KEY ACQUISITION (CLASSIC VACATIONS)**  
• Luxury travel wholesaler (NA-focused) acquired for ~$125mn  
• High-value bookings (12–14x TBO avg ticket size)  
• Improves take rates & premium mix  
• Synergies: cross-sell, tech integration, supply expansion  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹25.7bn → ₹42.3bn (FY26–28E)  
• EBITDA: ₹3.7bn → ₹7.4bn  
• EBITDA margin: ~14.5% → ~17.6%  
• PAT: ₹2.4bn → ₹5.2bn  
• RoE: ~18% → ~26%  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: 46x → 21x (FY26–28E)  
• EV/EBITDA: 26x → 13x  
• Premium justified by 30%+ EPS CAGR  
• Compared to global OTAs: smaller scale but higher growth  

**BALANCE SHEET & CASH FLOW**  
• Asset-light, negative working capital model  
• Strong FCF visibility (40%+ CAGR)  
• Operating leverage improves margins over time  

**MOAT & COMPETITIVE EDGE**  
• Strong supplier contracts & B2B pricing  
• Payment rails + credit + compliance infrastructure  
• Less dependent on Google/search vs B2C OTAs  
• AI disruption lower vs consumer travel platforms  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Hotel/ancillary mix scaling (>70%)  
• Classic Vacations integration success  
• Expansion into premium/luxury segment  
• Margin expansion via operating leverage  

**RISKS**  
• Travel demand volatility (geopolitics, macro)  
• Rising airfare/hotel costs impacting volumes  
• Integration risk (CV acquisition)  
• External shocks (climate, global disruptions)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• B2B travel infra play with strong network effects  
• Premiumization (luxury travel) = margin lever  
• Asset-light + global scale supports high ROE  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• TBO Tek is a structurally strong, asset-light global travel platform with high growth visibility, improving margins, and premium mix expansion—supporting a sustained re-rating cycle

**SYRMA SGS TECHNOLOGY: DESIGN-LED EMS WITH EXPORT & ODM TAILWIND**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• BUY call; TP ₹940 (~26% upside)  
• Strong growth visibility with improving margins  
• Positioned as design-led EMS, not pure assembly  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Diversified EMS player across consumer, auto, industrial, healthcare, IT, railways  
• Expanding into defence & ODM segments  
• 14 manufacturing plants + 4 R&D centres + 270+ clients  
• Presence across 25+ countries  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Rising exports (faster than domestic growth)  
• Increasing ODM contribution (higher margin)  
• Backward integration into bare PCB manufacturing  
• Diversified order book (~₹6,400 Cr visibility)  

**RECENT PERFORMANCE (Q3FY26)**  
• Revenue: +45% YoY (broad-based growth)  
• Exports: +66% YoY (mix improvement)  
• EBITDA margin: 12.6% (+350 bps)  
• PAT: ₹110 Cr (+108% YoY)  

**MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE**  
• FY26 revenue: ~₹5,000 Cr  
• FY27 growth: ~30% in revenue & EBITDA  
• EBITDA margin: ~10% (conservative vs current run-rate)  
• Export revenue: ~₹1,100 Cr (FY27E)  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE (ESTIMATES)**  
• Revenue: ₹4,827 → ₹8,150 Cr (FY26–28E)  
• EBITDA margin: ~10.5–10.6%  
• PAT: ₹305 → ₹529 Cr  
• EPS: ₹16 → ₹28  
• RoE: ~13% → ~15%  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• P/E: 49x → 28x (FY26–28E)  
• EV/EBITDA: 29x → 17x  
• Trading below historical avg P/E (~36x vs ~42x)  
• Premium justified by growth + mix improvement  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Low leverage (D/E ~0.12)  
• No promoter pledge  
• Strong execution capability with expansion runway  

**TECHNICAL VIEW**  
• Bullish trend; higher lows formation  
• RSI improving from mid-zone  
• Support from key moving averages  

**TRADE SETUP**  
• Entry: ₹760–790  
• Target: ₹940  
• Stop-loss: ₹650  
• Timeframe: 3–6 months  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Export scale-up  
• ODM share increase  
• PCB backward integration ramp-up  
• Defence & industrial segment expansion  

**RISKS**  
• High valuation dependence on sustained growth  
• Margin moderation vs current elevated levels  
• Execution risk in new segments  
• Global demand volatility impacting exports  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transitioning from EMS → design-led ODM player  
• Mix shift (exports + ODM) = margin expansion driver  
• Strong order visibility supports growth narrative  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Syrma is emerging as a high-growth, design-led EMS player with strong export momentum and improving margins, where sustained execution can justify premium valuations and drive continued re-rating

reports.text:
**URBAN COMPANY: STRUCTURAL PLAY, BUT VALUATION FULL**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Initiated with Neutral; TP ₹125 (~14% upside)  
• Strong structural tailwinds in home services  
• Valuation rich vs profitability profile  

**BUSINESS OVERVIEW**  
• Tech-enabled home services platform across 51 cities  
• Core segments:  
  • India consumer services (~76% revenue)  
  • Native (products like RO, locks)  
  • International (UAE, Singapore, Saudi)  
• FY25 NTV: ₹32.7bn; strong platform scale  

**INDUSTRY OPPORTUNITY**  
• India home services TAM: ~$60bn → ~$100bn by FY30  
• Online penetration <1% → large headroom  
• Highly fragmented offline market → structural shift opportunity  

**BUSINESS MODEL & MOAT**  
• Micro-market strategy → better utilisation & lower costs  
• Strong supply ecosystem (pros earn ~30–40% higher)  
• Network effects: density → better service → higher retention  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Rising online adoption in home services  
• Cross-category usage by existing customers  
• Native products (RO, locks) scaling rapidly  
• InstaHelp (optionality, early-stage demand)  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹15.3bn → ₹26.5bn (FY26–28E)  
• EBITDA: negative till FY27; improving to ~–5% margin in FY28  
• PAT: breakeven by FY28  
• RoE/RoCE: negative → improving trend  

**UNIT ECONOMICS & COHORTS**  
• Retained users contribute ~84% of NTV  
• Older cohorts show higher retention & spending  
• Disintermediation risk remains (offline shift post trust)  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Trades at high EV/Sales (~5.8x FY28E)  
• Premium vs internet & consumer peers  
• SoTP-based TP ₹125 (balanced risk-reward)  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Faster online penetration  
• Improved cohort retention & monetisation  
• Native segment scaling  
• InstaHelp turning profitable  

**RISKS**  
• Disintermediation (customer-pro direct connect)  
• High competition (pricing pressure, CAC rise)  
• Prolonged losses in InstaHelp  
• Slower shift from offline to online  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Large structural opportunity but execution-heavy  
• Profitability still evolving → key monitorable  
• Growth vs valuation mismatch currently  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Urban Company offers a strong long-term structural story in a large, underpenetrated market, but current valuations already price in much of the growth, limiting near-term upside

reports.text:
Ather and Ola Electric need to be appreciated here

They develop and control the IP for the 'brains' of the two wheeler after working on it completely in house

The Indian Diagnostics & Healthcare sector is at a major inflection point

Industry shifts  
- Routine testing commoditising; organized players pivoting to high‑value genomics, molecular & oncology  
- Continuous, preventive care replacing episodic testing; focus on B2C wellness platforms  

Key player moves  
- Dr Lal PathLabs: Launches ‘Sovaaka’ and scales ‘Swasthfit’ preventive packages  
- Metropolis: Drives ‘TruHealth’ wellness and Centre of Genomics in Delhi  
- Thyrocare: Expands ‘Aarogyam’ and ‘Jaanch’ lifestyle‑focused brands  

Margins & mix  
- Margin expansion mix‑driven, not price‑driven  
- 12% → 5% GST cut on reagents passed on to consumers, boosting volume  
- Operational leverage and richer test mix are main EBITDA drivers  

B2B & PPP strategy  
- Exiting low‑margin B2B hospital contracts; focusing on health‑tech aggregators and insurance tie‑ups  
- Heavy‑capex PPP MRI/CT centres inside government hospitals create long‑term, captive volumes  

AI & headwinds  
- AI currently limited to TAT improvement in radiology, workflow optimisation, and scientific upselling  
- One‑time margin hit from new Labour Wage Code provisions domestically  
- Exports‑facing labs and enzyme makers facing US‑tariff and China‑supply‑chain pressure  

Takeaway:  
Structural consolidation underway; national chains using genomics, PPP, and B2C wellness to derisk commoditised routine testing, making them better long‑term compounders than standalone, low‑margin labs.

The Bottom Line: A clear consolidation phase is underway. Standalone labs will fight a race-to-the-bottom on routine tests, while national organized chains are securing their future through complex genomics, preventive wellness ecosystems, and strategic health-tech integration

Hidden Headwinds & Global Disruptions. Domestically, the sector took a synchronized one-time 
margin hit from exceptional provisions related to the implementation of new Labor Wage Codes. 
Globally, export-heavy healthcare and enzyme manufacturers are battling severe margin pressures due to unpredictable U.S. tariffs and skyrocketing freight and supply chain costs out of China.

AI's Reality Check. Despite the widespread hype, AI isn't replacing pathologists overnight. Currently, its 
meaningful adoption is restricted to highly targeted use cases: drastically improving turnaround times 
(TAT) in CT/MRI radiology reporting, enabling scientific upselling, and optimizing workflow efficiencies.

The Heavy-Capital PPP Moat. While pure pathology expands via asset-light franchisees, some players 
are deploying heavy upfront capital (2.5x higher than routine pathology) into Public-Private Partnerships 
(PPP). Setting up MRI/CT centers inside government hospitals creates long-term captive volumes, decade-long revenue visibility, and exceptionally high entry barriers against regional competition.

The Strategic B2B Cleanup. Major players are ruthlessly exiting margin-dilutive, operationally 
inefficient institutional B2B accounts. The new B2B goldmines? Health-tech aggregators and insurance 
providers, where surging pre-policy and annual health check-ups are creating highly scalable, captive 
patient volumes

Margin Expansion is Mix-Driven, Not Price-Driven. Nobody is hiking prices to chase growth right 
now. In fact, recent government GST cuts on chemical reagents (from 12% to 5%) were proactively 
passed on to consumers to build brand trust and volume. Instead, EBITDA margin expansion is coming 
almost entirely from operational leverage and an enriched test mix.

The Shift from Episodic to Continuous Care. The Shift from Episodic to Continuous Care. 
Diagnostics is moving away from illness-driven testing toward personalized, preventive wellness. 
Companies are aggressively launching dedicated platforms to drive B2C volume and lock consumers into recurring annual testing cycles. For example: • Dr. Lal PathLabs recently launched 'Sovaaka' for 
personalized, science-led prevention and is scaling its 'Swasthfit' packages. • Metropolis Healthcare is 
driving its B2C growth through curated 'TruHealth' wellness packages. • Thyrocare Technologies is 
expanding its flagship 'Aarogyam' preventive brand alongside its 'Jaanch' lifestyle profiles

Genomics is the “New Black” in Pathology. With routine testing facing intense price commoditization, 
organized chains are aggressively pivoting toward high-end genomics, molecular diagnostics, and 
oncology. This complex testing demands precise interpretation and acts as a massive structural moat 
against unorganized, discount-heavy players.

The Indian Diagnostics & Healthcare sector is at a major inflection point. Based on Q3FY26 earnings calls, the era of fragmented, pure-play routine testing is giving way to a massive structural shift. Here are the trends, strategy pivots, and margin drivers reshaping the industry for institutional investors

**CHEMICAL SECTOR: COST SHOCK & MARGIN COMPRESSION PHASE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Brent >$100 + LNG/LPG disruption → sharp cost inflation  
• Freight disruption (Cape rerouting) → higher logistics cost  
• Sector entering margin compression cycle  

**COST PRESSURE DRIVERS**  
• Feedstock inflation (benzene, toluene, methanol, naphtha)  
• Higher fuel, freight & insurance costs  
• Export viability at risk for low-margin products  

**SECTOR DYNAMICS**  
• Commodity chemicals: most impacted (margin squeeze)  
• Specialty chemicals: relatively resilient (pricing power, contracts)  
• Export-oriented low-value products under pressure  

**PREFERRED STRATEGY**  
• Defensive stance  
• Focus on:  
  • High-value product mix  
  • Strong balance sheet  
  • Domestic-focused players  
  • Lower Middle East feedstock dependency

**KEY TRIGGERS**

• Correction in crude & freight costs

**RISKS**

Wires & Cables: Data Centre–Driven View

- India’s data centre capacity expected to reach ~8–10 GW by 2030  
- This alone can create a ~₹28,000–35,000 crore cables opportunity  
- Current C&W market size ~₹80,000–90,000 crore, so data centres are a meaningful incremental tailwind  

- Data centres are highly power‑intensive, requiring heavy cabling for power, backup, and cooling  
- Demand for high‑quality power, control, and specialized cables is rising structurally  

- Sector also benefits from T&D expansion, renewables, and the broader India capex cycle  

- For you, the theme works best via larger‑cap, high‑quality players (Polycab, Finolex, KEI, RR Kabel, Havells, V‑Guard, etc.) that have scale, EPC relationships, and balance‑sheet strength to service hyperscalers and large campuses.

Power Transmission Update: Execution vs Targets (FY25‑26 till Feb 28, 2026)

Transformation Capacity (MVA)  
- FY target: 115,127 MVA  
- Achieved: 91,408 MVA → 79.4% execution  
- Grand‑total FY target (including Feb): 126,007 MVA; Feb alone added 12,545 MVA  

By voltage – substation build‑out  
- 765 kV: 46,500 MVA vs 59,000 MVA target (78.8%)  
- 400 kV: 25,485 MVA vs 33,605 MVA (75.8%)  
- 230/220 kV: 19,423 MVA vs 22,522 MVA (86.2%) – best segment  

By sector – transformation  
- Central: 49,505 MVA vs 61,820 MVA (80.1%)  
- State: 29,713 MVA vs 35,062 MVA (84.8%) – best sector  
- Private: 12,190 MVA vs 18,245 MVA (66.8%) – weak link  

Transmission Lines (ckm)  
- Till‑Feb target: 13,401 ckm  
- Achieved: 9,287 ckm → 69.3%  
- Full‑FY target: 15,382 ckm; Feb added 1,895 ckm  

By voltage – lines  
- 765 kV: 3,958 ckm vs 7,018 ckm (56.4%) – biggest drag  
- 400 kV: 2,373 ckm vs 2,346 ckm (101.2%) – only over‑target segment  
- 230/220 kV: 2,956 ckm vs 4,037 ckm (73.2%)  

By sector – lines  
- Central: 4,209 ckm vs 5,385 ckm (78.1%)  
- State: 3,755 ckm vs 4,961 ckm (75.7%)  
- Private: 1,323 ckm vs 3,055 ckm (43.3%) – major laggard  

Investment translation (which players benefit)  
- Power Grid (PGCIL) – largest beneficiary in 765 kV and 400 kV substation & line build‑out; strong order pipeline and capex still intact despite 765 kV line lag.  
- Kalpataru Projects, KEC International – 400 kV over‑achievement, HVDC‑linked 400 kV / 765 kV lines, and state‑segment activity favour EPC contractors; 230/220 kV strength also supports urban and state‑level T&D work.  
- State‑DISCOMs & state‑owned entities – consistent ~75–85% execution in both segments improves case for capital‑spend visibility for vendors and contractors dealing with state utilities.  
- Private capex‑linked plays – weakest link; slower private‑sector line build suggests that private‑led T&D and some RE‑evacuation timelines may slip, compressing margin visibility for pure‑private‑T&D‑exposed names.  

Key takeaway:  
Capacity build‑up in 230/220 kV and state‑sector projects is robust, supporting steady order flow for Power Grid, Kalpataru, and KEC, while 765 kV and private‑sector gaps highlight execution risk and potential for FY‑end acceleration if capex is pushed harder.

**NAZARA TECHNOLOGIES: ACQUISITION-LED EARNINGS RE-RATING**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Upgraded to BUY; TP ₹336 (~42% upside)  
• Earnings sharply upgraded post BT Games + BP Systems acquisition  
• Transitioning into scaled global casual gaming platform  

**FINANCIAL PROFILE**  
• Revenue: ₹16.2bn → ₹40.5bn (FY25–28, ~27.7% CAGR)  
• EBITDA: ₹5.0bn → ₹6.4bn (FY27–28E)  
• EBITDA margin: ~9.4% → ~15.8–15.9%  
• PAT: sharp recovery to ₹2.75bn by FY28  

**EARNINGS RESET**  
• FY27/28 sales revised up by ~62% / ~85%  
• EBITDA up ~64% / ~74%  
• EPS up ~28% / ~57%  

**STRATEGIC ACQUISITION (BT + BP)**  
• Acquiring 50% stake (Spain-based gaming platform)  
• Portfolio: 17 games | 375mn downloads | 22mn MAUs  
• Strong ad-driven monetisation (~95% revenue)  

**BUSINESS MODEL ADVANTAGE**  
• BP Systems = proprietary user acquisition (UA) engine  
• 40–50% installs driven internally → lower CAC, higher LTV  
• AI-led game development (cycle reduced to ~2–3 months)  

**GROWTH VISIBILITY**  
• BT+BP revenue: ~₹12.1bn (FY27E) → ~₹18.6bn (FY28E)  
• EBITDA margin: ~18–20%  
• Casual gaming becomes core growth driver  

**VALUATION CONTEXT**  
• Trades ~31.6x FY28E EPS | ~12.9x EV/EBITDA  
• Re-rating driven by scale + improved margins  
• BT+BP valued at ~1.5x FY28E sales  

**BALANCE SHEET & RETURNS**  
• Funded via internal cash + earn-out structure  
• RoE/RoCE improving to ~7.5% / ~9% by FY28  
• Near-term FCF pressure due to investment phase  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Closure of BT/BP deal (2–3 months)  
• Scaling of casual gaming portfolio  
• UA engine integration across Nazara ecosystem  
• Opening BP platform to 3rd-party developers  

**RISKS**  
• Integration risk (large overseas acquisition)  
• Dependence on ad monetisation (Meta/Google policies)  
• Short-term FCF pressure  
• Execution risk in scaling new vertical  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Shift from fragmented gaming portfolio → platform-led model  
• UA + content + distribution integration = strong moat  
• Earnings visibility significantly improved  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Nazara is entering a new growth phase driven by a transformative acquisition, with sharp earnings upgrades, improving margins, and platform-level scalability supporting a potential re-rating cycle

**INDIA MACRO: OIL SHOCK RESET (EMKAY VIEW)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Brent assumption raised to ~$80/bbl (FY27E); spike to ~$125 in near term  
• GDP cut to 6.6% (–40 bps); CPI raised to 4.3% (+30 bps)  
• Clear shift: inflation ↑, growth ↓ (stagflation-lite risk)  

**EXTERNAL BALANCE IMPACT**  
• CAD/GDP: 1.3% → 1.7% (base case)  
• Stress case ($100 oil): CAD >2.4%, BoP deficit >$85bn  
• Extreme case ($130 oil): CAD ~3.7%, BoP deficit ~$145bn  

**ENERGY SHOCK TRANSMISSION**  
• ~90% oil import dependence  
• Strait of Hormuz critical:  
  • ~45–46% crude imports  
  • ~55% LNG imports  
• Gas curtailment already visible (~31% volumes cut)  

**OMC & FISCAL DYNAMICS**  
• OMC under-recoveries >₹3 lakh Cr annually  
• Required fuel price hikes:  
  • Diesel: ~42–43%  
  • Petrol: ~18–19%  
• Full govt absorption → fiscal hit ~1–1.1% of GDP  

**POLICY RESPONSE (BASE CASE)**  
• Shared burden model (Govt + OMCs + consumers)  
• OMC hit: ~0.35% of GDP  
• CPI impact: ~30–35 bps  
• Fiscal hit: ~0.4–0.5% of GDP  

**RBI & MARKETS**  
• INR depreciation likely tolerated (~USD/INR → 96)  
• 10Y G-Sec yield ~6.95%  
• Focus on FX intervention vs rate hikes  

**MACRO LINKAGES**  
• GCC exposure:  
  • ~38–40% remittances  
  • ~13% exports  
  • ~5–9% FDI  
• Risk of spillover via remittances & trade  

**SECTORAL IMPLICATIONS**  
• Negative:  
  • OMCs (margin uncertainty)  
  • Energy-intensive industries  
  • Fertilizers, consumption (cost pressure)  
• Positive:  
  • Upstream oil & gas  
  • Gas producers  
  • Exporters (INR depreciation benefit)  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Brent trajectory vs $80 base case  
• Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline  
• Fuel price hikes / subsidy decisions  
• RBI stance on INR & bond yields  

**RISKS**  
• Crude sustaining >$100/bbl  
• Deeper gas shortages → industrial slowdown  
• Fiscal slippage crowding out capex  
• Weakening remittances/export flows from GCC  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• India highly sensitive to oil shocks due to import dependence  
• Policy balancing act between inflation, growth & fiscal stability  
• Market direction hinges on oil normalization timeline  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Negative for macro stability | Mixed for equities  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• The oil shock creates a complex macro trade-off—while growth slows and external balances weaken, policy calibration and sectoral divergence (losers vs beneficiaries) will define market outcomes in the near to medium term

**IRAN CONFLICT: ENERGY SHOCK VS GROWTH SHOCK (MACRO STRATEGY)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Largest oil supply disruption via Strait of Hormuz  
• Markets pricing inflation shock, not full growth shock  
• Elevated energy prices → downside risk to global growth  

**MACRO IMPACT**  
• +10% oil → GDP ↓ ~0.1%+, CPI ↑ ~0.2pp  
• Europe & Asia ex-China most impacted  
• Growth slowdown + sticky inflation risk  

**ENERGY MARKET DYNAMICS**  
• Brent elevated; could normalize to ~$70 (4Q26) if disruption eases  
• LNG supply disruption delays global supply wave  
• Structural support to gas prices medium-term  

**POLICY RESPONSE**  
• Fed cuts delayed (Sep, Dec expected)  
• ECB/BoE likely “higher for longer”  
• Tools: SPR releases, sanctions easing, fiscal measures  

**MARKET POSITIONING**  
• Markets underpricing growth shock risk  
• If prolonged disruption:  
  • Equities & cyclicals vulnerable  
  • Bond yields may fall (growth scare)  
  • Safe havens (JPY) outperform  

**SECTORAL IMPLICATIONS**  
• Winners:  
  • Energy (oil & gas)  
  • Inflation hedges (commodities)  
  • Safe-haven assets  
• Losers:  
  • Cyclicals, global trade-sensitive sectors  
  • High energy-import economies  

**KEY TRIGGERS**  
• Strait of Hormuz flow data & convoy success  
• Oil/LNG price trajectory  
• Central bank policy shifts  
• Conflict duration & escalation signals  

**RISKS**  
• Prolonged disruption → oil spike beyond historical highs  
• LNG supply delays → sustained high gas prices  
• Policy/political uncertainty (US, Middle East)  
• Underestimated global growth slowdown  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Current market narrative incomplete (inflation-only view)  
• Growth shock risk is the real tail risk  
• Energy markets driving global macro direction  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Negative for global growth | Positive for energy assets  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• The Iran conflict represents a high-impact macro event where markets are underpricing growth risks—if energy disruptions persist, the narrative could shift from inflation shock to a broader global slowdown with significant implications for equities and asset allocation

• New PEB plant: 40,000 MTPA (Kheda, Gujarat)  
• Heavy structures unit: 25,000 MTPA (Andhra Pradesh)  
• Strengthens end-to-end execution capability  

**BUSINESS MODEL**  
• Converts HR/CR steel into I-sections, beams & components  
• Offers full turnkey PEB solutions (design → manufacturing → execution)  
• In-house accessories enhance integration and margins  

**SEGMENT DYNAMICS**  
• PEB: High volume, faster execution (40–50mm plates)  
• Heavy structures: Complex, high-load projects (~125mm plates)  
• Lower throughput but higher value-added applications  

**DEMAND DRIVERS**  
• Warehousing (e-commerce), FMCG, cement, industrial capex  
• New growth areas: data centres, semiconductors, multi-storey buildings  
• Strong order pipeline with large project wins  

**FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE**  
• Revenue CAGR ~32% (FY22–25)  
• EBITDA margin: ~4% → ~9%  
• PAT growth strong; ROE ~19–20%, ROCE ~19–26%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Net debt negative (cash-rich)  
• Strong free cash flow generation  
• Healthy EBITDA to OCF conversion  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Transition from pure PEB → higher-value heavy structures  
• Capacity expansion aligned with structural capex cycle  
• Integrated model + repeat orders = strong execution moat  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Interarch is evolving into a higher-value steel construction player, combining strong demand tailwinds with capacity expansion and a robust balance sheet, positioning it well for the ongoing infrastructure and industrial capex cycle

**ADITYA VISION: DEMAND-LED GROWTH WITH STORE EXPANSION**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• ICICI Sec maintains BUY; TP ₹600 (~31% upside from CMP ₹458)  
• Strong demand in RACs, fans, coolers → high-teen Q4 growth expected  
• Revenue/PAT CAGR (FY25–28E): ~19% / ~23%  

**DEMAND & CATEGORY TRENDS**  
• RAC: Strong demand + 7–9% price hike from Apr’26  
• LDA (fridge, WM, TV): Healthy traction; mix improving  
• Induction: Volume spike (gas shortage) but low earnings impact  
• Mobiles/Laptops: Weak due to chip shortage; price hikes ~5–7%  

**STORE EXPANSION**  
• 5 stores added in Q4; total ~197  
• On track to cross 200 stores by FY26 end  
• Expansion key driver for growth visibility  

**MARGIN & PROFITABILITY**  
• EBITDA margin stable at ~8.9%  
• Pre-stocked RAC inventory → near-term margin support  
• ROE improving: ~19.7% → ~21.3% (FY25–28E)  
• ROCE improving: ~13.4% → ~16%  

**BALANCE SHEET**  
• Net debt/EBITDA to decline: ~1.5x → ~0.6x  
• Strong asset turns (7.5x → 9.1x)  
• Improving free cash flow trajectory  

**RISKS**  
• Delay in store rollout  
• Input cost inflation / freight costs  
• Weak consumer demand or competition  
• Supply constraints (RAC, gas-related disruptions)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Growth driven by store expansion + summer demand cycle  
• Operating leverage + improving balance sheet supports valuation  
• Near-term margin tailwind from inventory positioning  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• Aditya Vision combines strong demand tailwinds with aggressive store expansion and improving financial metrics, offering a favorable growth trajectory with manageable risks

HOME SERVICES: PRICE WAR VS PROFITABILITY  

KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
• Platforms (Urban Company, Snabbit, Pronto) cutting prices aggressively  
• Services priced as low as ₹80–99; entry offers at ₹39  
• Strategy: acquire users first, profitability later  

UNIT ECONOMICS CHALLENGE  
• Worker income target: ~₹30,000/month  
• Platform breakeven: ~₹1,200/day (~₹200/hour per worker)  
• Current pricing makes sustainable margins difficult  

DEMAND & UTILISATION  
• Low prices improving demand and utilisation  
• Services are non-essential → demand remains inconsistent  
• Income reality (~₹20,000/month) below advertised levels  

PRICING CONSTRAINT  
• At ₹300–350/hour, demand may drop sharply  
• Limits ability to improve margins via price hikes  

STRUCTURAL LIMITATION  
• Labour-intensive model → limited scope for cost reduction  
• Unlike quick commerce, no major operating leverage  

STRATEGIC INSIGHT  
• Short-term growth driven by discounting  
• Long-term sustainability depends on pricing discipline + utilisation  

IMPACT ASSESSMENT  
• Negative (profitability concerns)  

OVERALL TAKEAWAY  
• Aggressive discounting is driving growth but weakening unit economics, making long-term profitability in home services platforms uncertain

India Has Just 25 Days of Oil Reserves. Here’s Why It Matters

Among major economies, India has one of the lowest strategic oil reserves at just 25 days.

Compare that with Japan at 254 days and the United States at 189 days. The gap is significant.

This highlights a key vulnerability. India depends heavily on crude oil imports, making it sensitive to global price shocks and supply disruptions.

**MUTUAL FUND SCREENING: RISK-ADJUSTED OUTPERFORMERS**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Focus on actively managed equity mutual funds  
• Emphasis on consistency + quality of returns (not just absolute returns)  
• Based on robust multi-factor evaluation framework  

**SCREENING CRITERIA**  
• Minimum 3-year performance track record  
• Sharpe Ratio > 1 (efficient risk-return trade-off)  
• Sortino Ratio > 1 (better downside risk management)  
• Positive Information Ratio (consistent benchmark outperformance)  
• Alpha > 1 (genuine excess return generation)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Filters out funds with volatile or unsustainable performance  
• Identifies process-driven fund managers with disciplined strategy  
• Focus shifts from returns → risk-adjusted wealth creation  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (for long-term portfolio construction)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• This framework highlights high-quality mutual funds that deliver consistent, risk-efficient returns—ideal for building long-term, stable wealth rather than chasing short-term performance

**POWER GRID: MOSL VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Neutral  
- Target Price: ₹302

**CAPEX & GUIDANCE UPDATE**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn; vs earlier ₹280 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn; vs earlier ₹200 bn)  
- FY27/28 Guidance unchanged:  
  • FY27: ₹300 bn / ₹350 bn  
  • FY28: ₹370 bn / ₹450 bn  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Capex based on current order visibility; further wins may drive upgrades  
- Strong multi-trillion transmission opportunity unfolding in India  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Improved execution (ROW resolution) supporting higher capitalization  
- Large pipeline ensures long-term growth visibility  
- Incremental upside dependent on new project awards  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Balanced risk-reward at current levels  
- Strong fundamentals but limited near-term rerating triggers  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **high-visibility utility growth story**, with capex momentum improving, but near-term upside depends on fresh order inflows and valuation comfort

- Additional optionality from storage and intra-state projects

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral

- Power Grid remains a **steady long-term infrastructure play**, with new opportunities emerging, but near-term upside constrained due to valuation and timing of growth execution

**VALUATION CONCERN**

- Limited upside despite strong fundamentals  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Negative

- Power Grid offers **high visibility, stable growth**, but elevated valuations cap near-term upside, leading to a cautious stance despite strong structural tailwinds

**COAL INDIA: NUVAMA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Reduce  
- Target Price: ₹384

- Limited ability to pass on higher costs  
- Earnings growth expected to remain muted  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**

- Reflects subdued growth and margin pressures  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Structural challenges from rising domestic supply  
- Pricing power constrained despite global tailwinds  
- Cost inflation (wages) adds further pressure  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Coal India faces a **low-growth, margin-constrained phase**, with limited upside as supply pressures and cost headwinds outweigh pricing benefits

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Initiates with Neutral rating  
- Target Price: ₹910  
- Large analytics TAM opportunity (~$328 bn)  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Revenue CAGR: ~20% (FY26–FY30)  
- EBIT CAGR: ~27%  
- Strong exposure to AI/ML and advanced analytics segments  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Trades at ~41x FY27 P/E  
- Premium valuation already pricing in strong growth expectations  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Positioned well in high-growth analytics and AI space  
- Strong earnings growth visibility, especially at EBIT level  
- However, upside limited due to already rich valuations  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Fractal Analytics offers strong structural growth in AI/analytics, but premium valuation caps near-term upside, leading to a balanced risk-reward outlook

**FRACTAL ANALYTICS: MORGAN STANLEY INITIATION**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Initiates with Overweight rating  
- Target Price: ₹964  
- Positioned as a high-growth challenger in tech services  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Strong positioning in data, analytics, and AI-led services  
- Platform-centric model enabling agentic AI delivery  
- Revenue growth expected to outpace industry peers  

**MARGIN & VALUATION VIEW**  
- High gross margins indicate quality business model  
- EBIT margin gap vs peers expected to narrow over time  
- Trades at premium P/E, justified by superior growth outlook  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Structural shift toward AI & analytics favors Fractal’s capabilities  
- Platform-led approach provides scalability and differentiation  
- Margin expansion is key monitorable for re-rating  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Fractal Analytics offers a compelling growth + AI play, with premium valuation supported by strong positioning and expected margin expansion trajectory

**APOLLO HOSPITALS: CITI VIEW UPDATE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹9,600  
- Recommendation: Buy (maintained)  
- Added to Citi’s Pan-Asia Focus List  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Preferred pick in Indian hospital space  
- Structurally resilient core hospital business  
- Fast-growing pharmacy segment with digital profitability in sight  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Hospitals provide stable, high-margin cash flows  
- Pharmacy + digital ecosystem offers scalable growth opportunity  
- Valuations below long-term averages (absolute & relative)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Apollo Hospitals offers a strong combination of defensive healthcare earnings and high-growth optionality, with valuation comfort supporting upside potential

**INDIGO: JEFFERIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹6,140  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Summer schedule provides improved visibility on capacity growth  

**CAPACITY & GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Domestic departures expected to grow ~6–7% YoY  
- Reported schedules slightly lower YoY, indicating calibrated expansion  
- Supports FY27 ASK growth estimate of ~10–11%  
- Still below historical ASK CAGR of ~13–15%  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Controlled capacity growth reduces execution and oversupply risks  
- Improved confidence in demand-supply balance  
- Earnings visibility now linked to fuel cost trajectory  

**KEY RISK**  
- Prolonged West Asia crisis → higher ATF (jet fuel) prices  
- Fuel inflation could pressure margins despite stable capacity growth  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (with fuel cost sensitivity)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- IndiGo’s disciplined capacity growth strengthens earnings visibility, but profitability remains highly sensitive to fuel prices amid ongoing geopolitical risks

**ETERNAL: KOTAK SECURITIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹375  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Platform fee hike implemented to counter rising costs  

**KEY DRIVERS**  
- Higher packaging costs impacting unit economics  
- Increase in average delivery distance per order  
- Potential petrol price hikes adding to cost pressure  
- Restaurants limiting menu → expansion of delivery radius  

**COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**  
- Swiggy yet to implement similar fee hike (may follow)  
- Industry may move towards higher pricing to sustain margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Platform fee hike expected to largely offset cost inflation  
- Helps maintain contribution margin (CM) at segment level  
- Structural shift towards higher delivery radius may increase cost base  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Timely pricing action positions Eternal to defend margins amid rising delivery and input costs, with potential industry-wide pricing normalization ahead

**BANKING SECTOR: NOMURA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Kotak Mahindra Bank upgraded to Buy; Target Price ₹445  
- System credit growth accelerated to ~14.9% YoY (vs ~10% earlier)  
- Sector facing rising funding costs and liquidity tightness  

**SECTOR TRENDS**  
- Credit-Deposit (CD) Ratio elevated at ~82% vs ~75% historical average  
- Liquidity buffers & LCRs declining across banks  
- Deposit mobilisation lagging credit growth  
- Government spending & FX inflows remain weak  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- Funding costs rising despite policy easing  
- NIM estimates revised downward across the sector  
- Profitability may face near-term pressure  

**STOCK PREFERENCES**  
- Top Picks: Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, ICICI Bank  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong credit growth is a positive, but sustainability depends on deposit growth  
- Higher CD ratio signals tighter liquidity → competition for deposits likely to intensify  
- Margin compression risk persists due to rising cost of funds  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral (Growth strong, but margins under pressure)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Banking sector is in a high-growth but tightening liquidity phase, where deposit mobilisation and cost of funds will be the key drivers for earnings going forward

**ASIAN PAINTS: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹2,126  
- Recommendation: Underweight  
- Price hikes of ~6–8% expected in two phases  

**KEY OBSERVATIONS**  
- Company aiming to minimize price hikes to protect demand  
- Customer sensitivity influencing pricing strategy  
- Thinners likely to see sharper price increases  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Calibrated price hikes indicate demand remains fragile  
- Limited pricing aggression may delay margin recovery  
- Product-level pricing (like thinners) used to partially offset cost pressures  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Despite planned price hikes, weak demand and cautious pricing approach may keep margin recovery slow, supporting a cautious stance on the stock

**ASIAN PAINTS: MACQUARIE VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹3,100  
- Recommendation: Outperform  
- Announces 6–8% price hike in two phases  

**KEY DRIVERS**  
- Strong pricing power across the industry  
- Price hikes aimed at passing on input cost inflation  
- Follows historical pattern of gradual price transmission  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Confirms ability of paint companies to protect margins via pricing  
- Input cost-led margin pressure likely temporary  
- Phased hikes reduce demand disruption risk  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Asian Paints’ pricing strength reinforces its leadership position, with margin headwinds seen as short-term and recoverable through calibrated price hikes

**APOLLO HOSPITALS: CITI VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹9,600  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Added to Citi’s Pan-Asia focus list  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Preferred pick in Indian hospital space  
- Structurally resilient core hospital business  
- Strong growth in pharmacy segment with digital profitability in sight  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Hospitals business provides stable cash flows and pricing power  
- Pharmacy + digital ecosystem offers long-term growth optionality  
- Valuations below long-term averages (both absolute & relative)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Apollo Hospitals combines defensive healthcare cash flows with high-growth pharmacy/digital play, making it a strong long-term compounder at reasonable valuations

**PAINT SECTOR: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Demand remains weak, especially in retail segment  
- Price hikes expected across players to support margins  
- Competitive intensity rising with higher discounts and incentives  

**PRICING & DEMAND TRENDS**  
- Asian Paints: No price hike until Mar 20; hike expected in early April  
- Berger Paints: Already implemented ~4–5% hikes in select solvent-based products (from Mar 21)  
- Industry: Gradual price hikes likely rather than sharp increases  

**COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**  
- Increasing aggression from JSW Paints & Akzo Nobel vs earlier periods  
- Higher dealer & painter incentives impacting margins  
- Asian Paints intensifying efforts to regain retail share (especially West India)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Weak demand + rising competition = pressure on volume growth  
- Price hikes may support margins but risk further demand slowdown  
- Elevated discounting suggests stress in maintaining market share  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Slightly Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Paint sector faces a tough near-term setup with weak demand and rising competition, where margin recovery depends on successful price hikes without hurting volumes

**CHEMICAL SECTOR: JEFFERIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Middle East conflict disrupting chemical feedstock supply chains  
- India heavily dependent on the region for key raw materials  
- Near-term inventory gains, but margin pressure ahead  

**SECTORAL IMPACT**  
- Most Impacted: Fertilizers  
- Other Affected: Plastics, Paints, Adhesives, Explosives, Consumer Durables  
- Refgas: Sharp input cost inflation → margin pressure  
- Crop Protection: Better positioned due to cost pass-through in export contracts  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Q4 (March quarter): Likely inventory gains due to rising prices  
- From April onwards:  
  • Operating rate cuts possible  
  • Margin compression if supply disruptions persist  
- Companies with pricing power or export-linked contracts relatively safer  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative (especially from Q1 FY27 onwards if disruption sustains)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Short-term gains may mask underlying stress, but prolonged conflict can trigger margin compression and production cuts across the chemical value chain

**ITC: GOLDMAN SACHS VIEW UPDATE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price cut to ₹330 (from ₹385)  
- Rating maintained at Neutral  
- Earnings estimates downgraded  

**KEY REASONS**  
- Calibrated price hikes instead of sharp increase to offset tax impact  
- Higher-than-expected pressure on cigarette margins  
- Input cost inflation impacting FMCG margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Gradual pricing strategy may protect volumes but delays margin recovery  
- Cigarette business (core profit driver) facing near-term profitability pressure  
- FMCG margin expansion outlook remains challenging  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Slightly Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- ITC faces near-term earnings pressure due to tax and cost headwinds, with limited upside until margin recovery visibility improves

UBS On ITC

Target Price ₹395

Recommendation Buy 

New prices should protect volumes

Adopted a three-pronged pricing strategy to protect volumes

ITC has ensured a same-price option across all sensitive variants

This should largely limit volume impact

Nonetheless, ITC is likely to exceed the bearish consensus expectations for FY27

Valuation is very appealing in our view

**ASK AUTOMOTIVE: CLSA MAINTAINS OUTPERFORM**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Outperform maintained  
- Target Price: ₹640  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Honda (HMSI) expanding capacity ~28% (6.25mn → 8mn units)  
- ₹1,500 Cr capex at Tapukara plant  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- ASK derives ~35% revenue from HMSI → strong beneficiary  
- Expansion supports higher component demand  
- EV transition tailwind: 30–50% higher content per vehicle vs ICE  

**OUTLOOK**  
- HMSI product push strengthens long-term growth visibility  
- ASK well positioned for next growth phase driven by EV + volume expansion  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- HMSI capacity expansion and EV shift create a strong structural growth tailwind for ASK Automotive

**ALLIED BLENDERS: NUVAMA MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹670  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Q3 weakness due to regulatory factors (Telangana destocking, Maharashtra policy)  
- Q4 expected to see recovery  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Premiumisation trend continues; ~50% mix by FY28 (vs 47% currently)  
- ABD Maestro (JV with Ranveer Singh) gaining traction with new launches  
- Focus on luxury positioning through premium portfolio  

**MARGINS & OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA margin expected to expand to ~17–18% by FY28 (vs 13.2%)  
- Supported by favourable raw material basket, India-UK FTA & backward integration  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong premiumisation strategy and margin expansion visibility support long-term growth despite near-term regulatory disruptions

**KALYAN JEWELLERS: MOSL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹550  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong demand in India despite rising gold prices  
- Near-term impact expected on MENA business due to geopolitical issues  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Continued aggressive store expansion in India  
- Candere (online segment) turns profitable with strong momentum  

**MARGINS & FINANCIALS**  
- EBITDA margin expansion driven by better mix & operating leverage  
- Deleveraging and lower finance costs to support profitability  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong domestic demand and improving profitability drivers outweigh near-term geopolitical risks

**STAR HEALTH: JEFFERIES MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹660  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Loss ratio expected to improve (~200 bps over FY25–28E)  
- Driven by resumed price hikes & selective underwriting  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Additional levers: claim automation, hospital partnerships, Public Insurance Registry  
- Strong earnings visibility with ~21% IFRS EPS CAGR (FY26–28E)  

**VALUATION**  
- Trading at ~18x FY27E P/E; does not fully reflect growth potential  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Improving profitability metrics and strong growth outlook make current valuations attractive

**PREMIER ENERGIES: DAM CAPITAL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,295  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong competitive moat driven by technology & execution capabilities  
- Superior positioning in solar cell manufacturing  

**COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE**  
- Best-in-class cell efficiency  
- Ability to commission & stabilise plants without tech tie-ups  
- Faster ramp-up (4–6 months vs 12–18 months for peers)  
- Strong in-house R&D (G12R cells, zero busbar cells)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Technological edge and execution strength support long-term growth  
- Difficult for peers to replicate capabilities  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong execution + innovation-led moat positions Premier Energies as a key beneficiary in solar manufacturing growth

**HDFC BANK: HSBC MAINTAINS BUY**

**DAM Capital Note – Key Highlights (Macro & Sector)**

**Macro & Commodities (Strait of Hormuz shock)**  
- Closure hits **~20% of global LNG seaborne trade**, **~32% of LPG seaborne exports** and **~10% of seaborne oil**  
- India is highly dependent on **Strait of Hormuz (SOH)** for **LNG, LPG and oil**  
- For India: each **1 USD/bbl above budgeted 70 USD/bbl** adds **~₹100bn to fiscal deficit**  
- Sustained **85–90+ USD/bbl crude** can push **CAD to 1.5–2% of GDP in Q1 FY27**, **CPI >4.5%** and **INR toward 95**  

**Oil Availability vs Product Bottlenecks**  
- **Crude and transport fuels (gasoline, diesel, ATF)** are “manageable” via **inventories, IEA release and Russian supply**  
- Real bottlenecks: **refined products, LNG, LPG, NGLs, methanol, sulphur, helium and aluminium** with **sharp price risk**  

**India – Availability & Pricing**  
- Roughly **okay on crude and transport fuels** thanks to domestic refining and diversified sourcing; **LPG and bitumen** most stressed  
  - LPG inventory ~15 days  
  - Long lead times from US  
- **Petrol/diesel pass‑through politically constrained** (election‑heavy year):  
  - OMCs initially absorb **marketing losses**  
  - **Excise cuts likely** if crude stays elevated  
- **External‑sector headwinds**:  
  - Higher **freight/insurance (potential 2x)**  
  - Longer routes via **Cape of Good Hope**  
  - Around **38% of India’s remittances** at risk from Gulf disruptions  

**Big Macro Conclusions**  
- **Near term**: macro fundamentals hold; stress appears first in **CAD, INR and subsidies** (oil, fertiliser)  
- **If war lasts >1 quarter with crude >100 USD/bbl**:  
  - **Inflation risk (CPI >4.5%)**  
  - **Pause in rate cuts**  
  - **Fiscal deficit 10–20 bps wider**  
  - **GDP growth downgrade (FY27 to ~6.8% vs 7%)**  

**Sector Winners (India – relative positives)**  
- **Upstream O&G (ONGC, Oil India)**:  
  - **~11–12% EBITDA uplift per 5 USD/bbl**  
  - Lower perceived **windfall‑tax risk** under ORDA framework  
- **RIL (Refining, petchem)**:  
  - **~2.5% EBITDA boost per 1 USD/bbl GRM gain**  
  - Spot GRMs >25 USD/bbl vs normal ~8 USD/bbl; petchem also improving  
- **Sugar**:  
  - Higher crude → **more Brazilian ethanol diversion** → **tighter global sugar**  
  - Better **prices and export opportunity** (0.6–1 MT vs 0.3 MT already exported)  
  - **India ethanol‑blending push** may accelerate  
- **Select agro‑input names** (fertilisers, sulphur‑linked) benefit from **inventory gains** and **better pricing** if disruption persists  

**Sector Losers (India – clear negatives)**  
- **OMCs (IOCL, BPCL, HPCL) & CGDs (IGL, MGL, GUJGAS)**:  
  - **~30–50% EBITDA hit per 5 USD/bbl** (no retail price hike)  
  - **CGDs**: lose **~₹2/scm of EBITDA (15–30%) without pass‑through**  
- **Airlines**:  
  - **ATF is 35–40% of cost**; **10% ATF rise cuts EBITDA ~15%**  
  - Also hit by **ME airspace closures and rerouting of long‑haul**  
- **Infra**:  
  - Higher oil squeezes fiscal space and **Middle East order flows**  
  - Risk to India’s **>₹12tn capex plan** if crude averages ~90 USD/bbl (~₹2tn fiscal hit)  
- **Cement**:  
  - No ME fuel sourcing, but **10% rise in petcoke/coal cuts EBITDA/t by ~8% (~₹130/t)**  
  - Polypropylene bag costs add another **~₹80/t**  
- **Tiles & bathware / wood panels**:  
  - Gas and resin cost inflation → **70–150 bps margin risk** for tiles, **30–60 bps** for wood panels on 10% energy/RM rise  
  - Added **volume pressure** from weak demand  

**Mixed / Time‑Dependent Impacts (India)**  
- **Fertilisers**:  
  - Near‑term **input‑cost/logistics shock**; not an immediate availability crisis  
  - **DAP** most exposed via imports, **urea** on gas/curtailment  
  - **SSP and NPK** better placed but face **sulphur/ammonia/phosphate cost risk**  
- **Chemicals**:  
  - Near‑term **inventory gains**, then **refilling at higher RM and freight costs**  
  - Most exposed chains: **methanol, ammonia, crude‑linked petrochemicals** (ethylene, propylene, benzene, toluene)  
- **FMCG**:  
  - **Crude‑linked packaging (PET, HDPE, laminates
